name: Polyclonal IgG Antibody (generic, dilute)
vendor_example: Jackson ImmunoResearch 111-005-003 (normal goat IgG reference), Abcam ab37355

sds_facts.storage: Store at −20°C for long-term, or 4°C for up to 4 weeks. Aliquot before first freeze to avoid repeated freeze-thaw. 50% glycerol storage buffer prevents freezing at −20°C.

bench_knowledge: Proteins in 50% glycerol stocks (enzymes, antibodies, recombinant carriers) stay liquid at −20°C but denature rapidly above ~4°C. The glycerol stock is viscous, requiring reverse pipetting to eliminate air bubbles. Vortexing creates a high-shear air–water interface that unfolds proteins.

Proteins, oligonucleotides, and antibodies at low concentrations lose significant material to non-specific adsorption onto standard polypropylene and polystyrene surfaces within 30–60 minutes, particularly below 100 µg/mL. LoBind surfaces have reduced hydrophobic character; adding BSA or another carrier protein competes for adsorption sites and stabilises the stock at very low concentrations.
